raw_consumer_statement:
Order # XXXX ( XXXX ) I. On XX/XX/XXXX, at approximately XXXX XXXX, I followed the merchants mandated " Happy Returns '' protocol by surrendering three items at an authorized XXXX XXXX location. I witnessed the agent scan the items into the merchants system using XXXX XXXX XXXX. Per the merchants " paperless '' return policy, no physical receipt was provided at the point of surrender. 
II. On XX/XX/XXXX, XXXXover 30 days after the return was perfectedthe merchant sent an automated notice claiming the return had " expired. '' By the time this notice was issued, the merchant had already purged the digital record of the XXXX scan from their consumer-facing portal, effectively destroying the only evidence of my compliance with their return policy. I attempted to contact XXXX multiple times to confirm my refund, waiting over an hour in chat queues with no human response. So, on XX/XX/XXXX I initiated the American Express dispute. 
III. American Express reinstated the charge of {$98.00} on XX/XX/XXXX. The " proof '' submitted by XXXX to defeat the disputeand accepted by American Expressexpressly listed the order status as " NOT SHIPPED. '' American Express accepted " Not Shipped '' status as valid proof of a delivered and billable purchase, ignoring the fundamental contradiction in the merchants own records. 
IV. I appealed the reinstatement on XX/XX/XXXX and it was reinstated on XX/XX/XXXX. I tried multiple times to resolve this with the merchant, but for almost a month I was stonewalled. On XX/XX/XXXX, I initiated another formal appeal with XXXX XXXX uploaded extensive supporting documentation to the American Express portal. On XX/XX/XXXX, an American Express agent admitted via chat that uploaded documents require 48 hours to " reflect '' on an account. Nevertheless, American Express summarily closed my appeal in less than 24 hours. This rapid closure, occurring before the documents were even viewable by an adjuster, constitutes a failure to conduct a " reasonable investigation '' under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( 15 U.S.C. 1666 ) and a deceptive act under New York General Business Law 349.
